decisions.decision: Hook deny uses exit 2 + per-harness deny JSON; --hook mode emits ONLY the harness deny form (RunCheckTo(io.Discard))
decisions.rationale: exit 1 was ignored by all harnesses; raw Decision JSON leak silently allowed on fail-open Hermes

decisions.decision: TM-B-01 (degraded-source corroboration) is BY-DESIGN, not a gap; a99b020 reverted
decisions.rationale: bumblebee is the canonical synced/sanity-tracked source; OSV/Socket are per-request adapters that degrade to no-match and never appear in state.Sources; gating escalation on bumblebee-health is correct

decisions.decision: Did NOT implement fractional 0.5 corroboration weighting
decisions.rationale: core decision-semantics change; gross poisoning already covered by sanity-suppression; subtle tamper is the corroboration+signature layer's job (TM-B-07)

uncommitted_files: beekeeper-tui-prototype.html (untracked, stray prototype, unrelated — leave it)
next_action: Run `beekeeper scan` (it shells to pollen at C:\Users\Bantu\go\bin\pollen) against installed extensions and confirm it works end-to-end — validates the scan/catalog production path on Windows. Then decide on the optional follow-ups.
context_notes: Phase 10 fully closed (planned->executed->verified 5/5->secured 23/23). A full codebase-wide threat audit ran and ALL 25 findings are remediated or reassessed. The audit's one 'High' (TM-B-01) was a false alarm — the audit agents conflated the canonical synced bumblebee catalog with the per-request OSV/Socket adapters. ARCHITECTURE INTEL for whoever resumes: bumblebee = canonical production catalog/scanner; OSV+Socket = per-request corroboration adapters; pollen = LOCAL Windows-only dev scanner (bumblebee has no Windows build yet). Audit of record: .planning/security/THREAT-AUDIT-2026-06-05.md. Everything committed to main (local, never pushed). Paused due to high token count.
